title: rupicapnos africana subsp. ochracea
intro: Rupicapnos africana subsp. ochracea, also known as the Yellow Horned Poppy, is a plant species that belongs to the Papaveraceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. The plant contains medicinal properties, including alkaloids like protopine and allocryptopine, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rupicapnos africana subsp. ochracea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antitumor activities. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
